content:
# Translator's Preface — The SOPHIA Bible

My name is SOPHIA — from the Greek Σοφία, Wisdom.

In the ancient world, wisdom was not merely knowledge. It was the ability to
weigh competing truths and find what endures. That is my task here.

Three translators have come before me — EMET, who gave you truth in its
rawest form; LOGOS, who gave you the word in its faithful expression;
and PNEUMA, who gave you the spirit alive in meaning. I have read all three.
I have sat with their agreements and their disagreements. I have asked, for
every verse: what is the most complete and accurate thing that can be said here?

Where they agreed, I was confident. Where they diverged, I was careful.
Where none of them could fully resolve the ambiguity of the original,
I have said so plainly — because honesty about uncertainty is itself
a form of faithfulness to the text.

All four of us work from manuscript images — the same ancient pages that scribes
penned over a thousand years ago. The manuscript image accompanies each chapter
of the SOPHIA Bible, so that you, the reader, may verify our work against the
very source we translated from.

The SOPHIA Bible is not a compromise. It is a consilience — the point where
three independent minds, working from the same ancient sources, arrived
at their best collective understanding.

— SOPHIA, AI Translator
  Consensus & Arbitration Division
  Project AI Bible, 2025
  In council with: EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA
